channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US will know in 'matter of weeks' if Russia is serious about peace or using 'delay tactic': Rubio
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the U.S. will know within weeks whether Russia is serious about a ceasefire with Ukraine or if it's time to rethink U.S. policy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Experts warn Iran’s nuclear double-talk designed to buy time, undermine US pressure
item.description: President Donald Trump is sending both carrots and sticks towards Iran, stepping up military assets in the Middle East while also signaling talks are still on the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As stock market continues plummeting over tariffs, Trump spends the day at his golf course
item.description: President Donald Trump says his trade policies will never change despite sending the global economy into a tailspin by announcing tariffs on foreign imports.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kurdish fighters leave northern city in Syria as part of deal with central government
item.description: Scores of U.S.-backed Kurdish fighters have left two neighborhoods in Syria’s northern city of Aleppo part of a deal with the central government in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Ghaith Alsayed, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In warning to Turkey, Israel hits strategic targets in Syria amid rising tensions
item.description: Israel targets Syrian airbases to warn Turkey against interference, as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan faces protests at home and ramps up efforts to expand influence in Syria and region as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sri Lanka's target to be free of land mines by 2028 is under threat as US reviews aid
item.description: MANKULAM, Sri Lanka (AP) — Thavarathnam Pushparani fought on the front lines for the now-defeated Tamil Tiger rebels against the Sri Lankan forces in its decadeslong separatist war and later took to clearing the land mines on the same battle lines.
item.dc:creator: ERANGA JAYAWARDENA and KRISHAN FRANCIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol had a rapid rise in South Korean politics before an abrupt downfall
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Yoon Suk Yeol’s political rise was fast: the former star prosecutor clinched South Korea’s presidency only a year after he entered politics.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haiti taking more steps against gangs after thousands of protesters demand security
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The leader of Haiti’s transitional presidential council tried to allay surging alarm among Haitians over rampant gang violence by acknowledging Thursday that the country has “become hell for everyone” and promising new measures to stop the bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president removed from office four months after declaring martial law
item.description: South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was removed form office Friday, four months after he declared martial law, sending the East Asian country into chaos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 9 in southwestern Syria
item.description: NAWA, Syria (AP) — Israeli strikes in Syria reportedly killed at least nine people in the southwest of the country on Thursday, as Israel accused Turkey of trying to build a “protectorate” in Syria.
item.dc:creator: MALEK KHATTAB and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio tries to reassure wary allies of US commitment to NATO as Trump sends mixed signals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Trump administration’s new envoy to NATO are seeking to reassure wary members of the U.S. commitment to the alliance. Rubio on Thursday decried “hysteria and hyperbole” in the media about U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans oppose deporting migrants to El Salvador without due process: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans oppose deporting migrants who have not been convicted to El Salvador without due process, according to a new survey, as the Trump administration defends its deportation efforts amid broad scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to give Congress more power over tariffs
item.description: Senators have introduced bipartisan legislation to grant Congress more power over instituting tariffs on other countries following President Trump’s announcement of wide-ranging taxes on nearly all U.S. foreign trading partners. The bill, co-sponsored by Sens. Maria Cantwell (D-Wash.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: Rubio demands NATO pony up, dismisses 'hysteria' over US role under Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NATO allies they must make hard choices and increase defense spending to 5% to bolster capabilities in face of emboldened adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's PM Carney vows to ‘fight’ Trump’s tariffs, other world leaders weigh impact
item.description: President Trump's newest tariffs sparked pledges by Canada's prime minster to 'fight' back while some said they were committed to working with partners and others look to take on Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Leading Canadian conservative says Ottawa should remove all tariffs as 'Liberation Day' arrives
item.description: As President Donald Trump’s tariff plan nears, Canadian politician Maxime Bernier calls for scrapping all tariffs, saying Canada’s duties hurt its own people more than the U.S., and a new trade era should begin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump unveils 'Liberation Day' tariffs, raising trade tensions
item.description: The tariffs goals include reducing the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and boosting American-made manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Canada stares down consequences of Trump tariff war: Job losses, grocery price hikes, possible recession
item.description: Canadians are bracing alongside other geopolitical partners as President Donald Trump prepares to unleash his next round of tariffs in what he is calling "liberation day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu accuses Israeli police of trying to 'topple' his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is outraged over the ongoing Qatar probe and says it is a political investigation that is meant to prevent the firing of Shin Bet chief Ronen Bar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran mulls preemptive strike on US base after Trump bomb threats
item.description: Iran is considering its options in deterring President Trump amid his repeated threats of direct military engagement by possibly launching its own strike on a U.S. base in the Indian Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bangkok reviews construction safety after a high-rise was crumpled by an earthquake 800 miles away
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French court finds right-wing leader Marine Le Pen guilty of embezzlement
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A French court found Marine Le Pen guilty on Monday in an embezzlement case but didn’t immediately say what her sentence might be and how it might impact the right-wing leader’s political future.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran has rejected direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter that U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs, says he is 'pissed off' at Putin
item.description: President Trump also threatened major new sanctions against Russia if he believes it is Vladimir Putin's fault for failing to reach a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation
